name: Nigerian Jollof Rice
description: A classic, vibrant West African rice dish cooked in a spicy, flavorful tomato-bell pepper broth.
instructions: Blend tomatoes, bell peppers, habanero peppers, and one onion. Boil to reduce excess water.
Sauté sliced onion in hot vegetable oil for 2 minutes, then fry tomato paste for 5 minutes.
Add boiled pepper blend and cook for 10 minutes until oil separates.
Stir in chicken broth, curry, thyme, bay leaves, and salt. Bring to a boil.
Add washed rice. Cover tightly with foil and a lid, and cook on low heat for 30 minutes.

name: Egusi Soup
description: A rich, savory soup made from ground melon seeds, palm oil, spinach, and assorted meats, traditional in Nigerian homes.
ingredients: 2 cups chopped spinach or pumpkin leaves (ugwu)
instructions: Boil assorted meats and stockfish with onions, seasoning cubes, and salt until tender. Save the stock.
Mix ground Egusi with a little warm water to form a thick paste.
Heat palm oil in a pot, add sliced onion, and fry for 2 minutes.
Add the Egusi paste in small lumps to the oil. Stir and fry on low heat for 10-15 minutes until fried.
Pour in the meat stock and boiled meats. Simmer for 15 minutes.
Add crayfish, pepper, and seasoning. Stir well and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the chopped spinach or ugwu leaves. Simmer for 3 minutes and remove from heat.

name: Spicy Beef Suya
description: Thinly sliced beef skewered and coated in a spicy, nutty peanut-chili spice blend (Yaji), grilled to perfection.
ingredients: 1/2 cup Yaji (Suya spice blend - ground peanuts, ginger, garlic, chili, paprika)
instructions: Slice the beef into very thin, wide strips.
Thread the sliced beef onto the soaked skewers in a zig-zag pattern.
Generously coat the skewered beef with the Yaji spice blend on both sides. Press the spice into the meat.
Drizzle vegetable oil over the skewers to keep them moist.
Preheat grill or oven to 200°C (400°F). Grill skewers for 10-12 minutes, turning halfway, until slightly charred.
Serve hot with extra Yaji spice, sliced onions, tomatoes, and cucumbers.

name: Akara (Bean Cakes)
description: Crispy on the outside, fluffy on the inside fried bean cakes made from blended peeled black-eyed peas, onions, and pepper.
instructions: Soak beans in water for 10 minutes, rub together to peel the skins, and rinse thoroughly to remove all skins.
Blend the peeled beans with the onion, habanero pepper, and minimal water to form a very thick paste.
Pour the paste into a bowl and whisk vigorously with a wooden spoon for 5-10 minutes to incorporate air (this makes it fluffy).
Stir in salt just before frying.
Heat oil in a deep pan. Scoop spoonfuls of the batter into the hot oil.
Fry on medium heat until golden brown on both sides. Drain on paper towels and serve warm.

name: Efo Riro (Yoruba Spinach Stew)
description: A rich, savory spinach stew cooked with palm oil, locust beans (iru), bell peppers, stockfish, and assorted meats.
instructions: Blanch spinach leaves in boiling water for 1 minute, drain, squeeze out excess water, and set aside.
Coarsely blend bell peppers and Scotch bonnets. Boil to dry out excess water.
Heat palm oil in a pot, add sliced onions and locust beans, and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add boiled pepper mix and cook for 10 minutes until oil rises to the top.
Add cooked meats, stockfish, crayfish, seasoning cubes, and salt. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add the blanched spinach. Stir thoroughly to combine, simmer for 3 minutes, and serve.

name: Goat Meat Pepper Soup
description: A light, fiery, and highly aromatic broth made with goat meat, scented leaves, and local pepper soup spices.
ingredients: A handful of fresh scent leaves or basil, chopped
instructions: Wash goat meat thoroughly and place in a pot.
Add chopped onions, seasoning cubes, salt, and a little water. Cook for 10 minutes.
Pour in 4 cups of water, add pepper soup spices, crayfish, and chopped peppers.
Cover the pot and boil on medium heat for 30 minutes, or until the goat meat is completely tender.
Add the chopped scent leaves and simmer for an additional 3-5 minutes. Serve hot.
